mission.title: Install and Configure Elasti (KubeElasti) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Elasti is a Kubernetes-native scale-to-zero solution that allows services to scale down during periods of low or no traffic without losing requests. It uses a lightweight resolver proxy to hold incoming requests while scaled-down workloads are brought back up, optimizing resource usage and reducing costs.

steps.title: Install Elasti using Helm (OCI)
steps.description:
Install Elasti from the OCI Helm registry into a dedicated namespace:
```bash
helm install elasti oci://tfy.jfrog.io/tfy-helm/elasti --namespace elasti --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Elasti operator and resolver pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n elasti
```
You should see the `elasti-operator` and `elasti-resolver` pods in a Running state.

steps.title: Verify CRDs
steps.description:
Confirm that the Elasti Custom Resource Definitions are registered:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep elasti
```
You should see `elastiservices.elasti.truefoundry.com`.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the elasti-operator and elasti-resolver pods running in the elasti namespace. The ElastiService CRD will be available for configuring scale-to-zero behavior on your workloads.

uninstall.title: Remove ElastiService resources and Helm release
uninstall.description:
Delete any ElastiService custom resources first, then uninstall the Helm release:
```bash
kubectl delete elastiservices.elasti.truefoundry.com --all --all-namespaces
helm uninstall elasti -n elasti
kubectl delete namespace elasti
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Elasti
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Helm release from the OCI registry:
```bash
helm upgrade elasti oci://tfy.jfrog.io/tfy-helm/elasti --namespace elasti
kubectl get pods -n elasti
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n elasti -l app.kubernetes.io/name=elasti-operator --tail=50
```

troubleshooting.title: Resolver not intercepting traffic
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the ElastiService resource is configured correctly and the resolver service exists:
```bash
kubectl get elastiservices --all-namespaces
kubectl get svc -n elasti
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Helm 3 installed. A Prometheus installation is recommended for metrics. An Ingress Controller is needed if using ingress-based routing with ElastiService.
